arbiter.rationale: The static evidence supports a run-level tool-extension registry finding and its effect on agent construction. The audit appropriately identifies mutable global propagation as a risk and recommends a fresh immutable run-scoped design rather than adopting the source mechanism.

governor.rationale: Mandatory Apache-2.0 review is complete for concept-only governance treatment. No Strix registry code, source expression, prompt, payload, exploit, media, documentation expression, or implementation is approved for reuse. Any future adaptation requires separate Apache-2.0 notice, NOTICE, attribution, patent, distribution, and third-party dependency review.

steward.rationale: Run-scoped capability registration aligns with Orchestra least-privilege, provenance, and deterministic orchestration goals when redesigned as an immutable authority-controlled manifest. It may advance as a proposal concept without adopting mutable global registry behavior.

maintainer_decision.rationale: Approve the capability-registry concept for later proposal consideration under a strict concept-only boundary. A future Orchestra-native design must use immutable run-scoped manifests, explicit provenance, role-based allowlists, deterministic validation, least privilege, and auditable capability grants.
